title: HTML Formatting
content:
### HTML Text Formatting HTML provides tags for **formatting** text with special meaning. --- ### Formatting Tags | Tag | Description | Example | |-----|-------------|---------| | `` | Bold text | `Bold` | | `` | Important text (bold) | `Important` | | `` | Italic text | `Italic` | | `` | Emphasized text (italic) | `Emphasized` | | `` | Highlighted text | `Marked` | | `` | Smaller text | `Small` | | `` | Deleted text (strikethrough) | `Deleted` | | `` | Inserted text (underline) | `Inserted` | | `` | Subscript | `H2O` | | `` | Superscript | `102` | --- ### Bold vs Strong Both make text **bold**, but: - `` -- just visual bold - `` -- adds **semantic importance** (screen readers emphasize) ```html This is bold This is important ``` ### Italic vs Emphasized Both make text *italic*, but: - `` -- just visual italic (e.g., technical terms, foreign words) - `` -- adds **semantic emphasis** --- ### Example ```html

This is bold and this is strong.

This is italic and this is emphasized.

This is highlighted text.

H2O is water. 102 = 100.

``` > **Best Practice:** Use `` and `` instead of `` and `` when you want to convey meaning, not just style. Screen readers will announce them differently.
codeExample:
Bold text

Important text

Italic text

Emphasized text

Highlighted text

Smaller text

This is deleted and inserted.

H2O (water) and 102 = 100

quiz.question: Which tag adds semantic importance (bold with meaning)?
